name: Pipeline Integration

tasks.title: Extend ContextBuilder to inject knowledge file content
tasks.description: Extend ContextBuilder to inject knowledge file content
tasks.body:
Add a new section to ContextBuilder.Build that injects the codebase knowledge file content. Per the spec, this section appears after universal/class guidance (current step 4b) and before prior task AARs (current step 5).

Implementation:
1. ContextBuilder.Build needs access to the wolfcastle directory path and the current namespace to locate the knowledge file. Add these as parameters to Build, or pass them through the config/identity. Check how identity (namespace) flows through the daemon to determine the cleanest approach.
2. Call internal/knowledge.Read(wolfcastleDir, namespace) to get the file content
3. If content is non-empty, emit it under a '## Codebase Knowledge' header
4. If no knowledge file exists or it's empty, omit the section entirely (no empty header)
5. The file is read every iteration, never cached, so updates from one task are immediately visible to the next

Update the ContextBuilder contract spec (2026-03-18T23-11Z-contextbuilder-contract.md) to document the new section in the assembly order.

Update existing ContextBuilder tests to verify knowledge injection in the correct position.
constraints: Do not cache the knowledge file content; read it fresh every Build call
acceptance_criteria: ContextBuilder.Build includes knowledge content between class guidance and AARs when a knowledge file exists

tasks.title: Add daemon maintenance task creation for knowledge budget overflow
tasks.description: Add daemon maintenance task creation for knowledge budget overflow
tasks.body:
Add a daemon check that creates a maintenance task when a knowledge file exceeds its token budget. Follow the pattern established by spec_review.go for maintenance task creation.

Implementation:
1. Add a method (e.g., checkKnowledgeBudget) that runs during the daemon's iteration loop
2. Read the knowledge file for the current namespace using internal/knowledge.Read
3. Count tokens using internal/knowledge.TokenCount
4. If token count exceeds the configured knowledge.max_tokens, create a maintenance task on the current node:
   - Title: 'Prune codebase knowledge file'
   - Body: instructions to review the knowledge file, remove stale entries, consolidate related entries, and bring it under budget. Include current token count and budget limit.
   - Insert before the audit task using the state.MoveAuditLast pattern
5. Only create the maintenance task once (don't create duplicates). Check for existing maintenance tasks with a known TaskType or title pattern before creating.
6. Log the maintenance task creation

Add tests covering: maintenance task creation on overflow, no duplicate creation, no task when within budget.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon creates a maintenance task when knowledge file exceeds budget

tasks.title: Update execute.md prompt with knowledge file instructions
tasks.description: Update execute.md prompt with knowledge file instructions
tasks.body:
Add an instruction to the execute.md prompt telling agents to update the knowledge file when they discover something non-obvious about the codebase. Per the spec, the instruction should read:

'If you discover something about this codebase that would be useful to future tasks and is not documented elsewhere (in the README, CONTRIBUTING.md, specs, or ADRs), append it to the codebase knowledge file using wolfcastle knowledge add "<entry>".'

Place this instruction in the appropriate section of execute.md (likely in the Record or Document phase, after the main implementation work). The instruction belongs in execute.md (system mechanics), not in class prompts. Keep it concise; agents should understand when and how to use it from a single paragraph.
acceptance_criteria: execute.md contains the knowledge file update instruction

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Pipeline Integration is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify knowledge section appears in ContextBuilder output between class guidance and AARs, not elsewhere
Verify daemon does not create duplicate maintenance tasks for the same knowledge file overflow
Verify ContextBuilder reads the knowledge file fresh each call, never caches it

scope.description: ContextBuilder knowledge injection (context_builder.go), daemon knowledge maintenance hook (knowledge_maintenance.go, knowledge_maintenance_test.go), execute.md Phase G addition, ContextBuilder contract spec update

breadcrumbs.text: Extended ContextBuilder to inject knowledge file content between class guidance and AARs. Added wolfcastleDir to struct, namespace to Build signature. Updated daemon caller, contract spec, and 4 new tests.

Added knowledge_maintenance.go with checkKnowledgeBudget daemon hook. Creates pruning task when knowledge file exceeds configured token budget. Seven tests, no duplicates, inserts before audit task. Hooked into RunOnce after spec review check.
Added Phase G (Capture Codebase Knowledge) to execute.md prompt template. Single paragraph instructing agents to use wolfcastle knowledge add for non-obvious codebase discoveries.

Audit: Reviewed 5 files (~520 lines of production/test code). PASS. Build, tests (with -race), vet, and gofmt all clean. Phase 1 findings: (1) knowledge_maintenance.go:78 uses generic fmt.Errorf('duplicate') rather than a sentinel error for the double-check inside MutateNode. Accepted: the error is intentionally swallowed at line 106, only one consumer, and the AAR already flagged this. Not worth the added type for a single call site. (2) ContextBuilder contract spec (line 119) says 'Build does not return an error' but Build returns error when taskID not found. Pre-existing inconsistency, not introduced by this node. Escalating. (3) Slice insertion in knowledge_maintenance.go:99-100 uses nested append, which is correct (no aliasing) but slightly harder to read than a manual copy. Accepted: follows patterns elsewhere in the codebase. (4) All four knowledge tests in context_builder_test.go verify content, omission, namespace guard, and ordering. Seven daemon tests cover overflow, dedup, within-budget, no-file, no-namespace, insert-before-audit, and missing-node. Coverage is thorough. (5) Execute.md Phase G is well-placed and concise. Phase lettering (G through K) is consistent. Quality: clean, well-tested work. Each task followed established patterns (ClassRepository for context_builder, spec_review for maintenance hook). The knowledge section reads fresh every Build call, which is correct since knowledge files change between iterations.

audit.result_summary: Pipeline integration delivered three changes: (1) ContextBuilder.Build now injects codebase knowledge file content between class guidance and AARs, reading fresh from disk every iteration via knowledge.Read, with the wolfcastleDir field on the struct and namespace parameter on Build. (2) The daemon gained a checkKnowledgeBudget hook that runs after each iteration and queues a maintenance task when the knowledge file exceeds the configured token budget, following the existing spec_review pattern with duplicate prevention via TaskType matching and MutateNode double-check. (3) The execute.md prompt template gained Phase G instructing agents to use wolfcastle knowledge add for non-obvious codebase discoveries. All work is backed by 11 new tests (4 in context_builder_test.go, 7 in knowledge_maintenance_test.go), builds clean with race detector, and the ContextBuilder contract spec was updated to document the new parameters and assembly step.

audit.objective: Verify all pipeline-integration work is complete and correct
audit.what_happened: Read all five files touched by the three tasks (context_builder.go, knowledge_maintenance.go, knowledge_maintenance_test.go, context_builder_test.go, execute.md) plus the updated ContextBuilder contract spec. Ran build, tests with race detector, go vet, and gofmt. All green. Walked through Phase 1 (instinct read) and Phase 2 (rubric) systematically. Found one pre-existing spec inaccuracy (escalated) and two minor accepted findings.
went_well: The three tasks followed consistent patterns: knowledge injection mirrors class guidance handling, maintenance hook mirrors spec_review pattern, tests cover both happy and edge paths thoroughly
Race detector clean confirms the no-cache design in knowledge reads is concurrency-safe
improvements: The escalated spec inaccuracy about Build error behavior should be fixed to prevent confusion for future readers
action_items: Fix ContextBuilder contract spec Error Behavior section to document that Build returns an error when taskID is not found

task-0001.objective: Inject codebase knowledge file content into ContextBuilder.Build output
task-0001.what_happened: Added wolfcastleDir field to ContextBuilder struct, namespace parameter to Build method. Knowledge section (step 4c) reads from knowledge.Read() every call, emitted under ## Codebase Knowledge header between class guidance and AARs. Updated daemon caller to derive namespace from config identity. Updated ContextBuilder contract spec with new section, parameters, and struct definition. Added four tests covering: content injection, omission when empty, omission when namespace empty, and ordering verification.
went_well: Clean separation: wolfcastleDir on struct, namespace on Build. No caching needed since knowledge.Read handles the filesystem.
Ordering test (KnowledgeBetweenClassAndAARs) validates the exact position in the assembly pipeline.
improvements: The replace_all edit for Build callers in near_threshold_coverage_test.go caught non-Build calls that also ended with ', nil)'. Had to manually revert three lines.
action_items: Execute prompt (execute.md) still needs the instruction for agents to update knowledge files, covered by a separate task.

task-0002.objective: Add daemon maintenance task creation when knowledge file exceeds token budget
task-0002.what_happened: Created knowledge_maintenance.go with checkKnowledgeBudget method following the spec_review.go pattern. The method reads the knowledge file via knowledge.Read, checks token count against config.Knowledge.MaxTokens, and creates a maintenance task with TaskType 'knowledge-maintenance' if over budget. Duplicate prevention uses TaskType matching. Task is inserted before the audit task using MoveAuditLast. Hooked the check into RunOnce after checkSpecReviewNeeded.
went_well: Clean parallel to the spec_review pattern: same MutateNode, same insert-before-audit, same logging structure
Seven tests cover: creation on overflow, no duplicates, within budget, no file, empty namespace, insert ordering, missing node
improvements: The double-check inside MutateNode returns a generic 'duplicate' error that gets silently swallowed. A sentinel error would be cleaner but isn't worth the added type for this case
action_items: The checkKnowledgeBudget call in RunOnce fires after every iteration regardless of outcome. If this becomes a performance concern, it could be gated on task completion only

task-0003.objective: Add knowledge file update instruction to the execute.md prompt template
task-0003.what_happened: Added a new Phase G (Capture Codebase Knowledge) to execute.md between the Document phase (F) and the Commit phase (H). The instruction tells agents to use wolfcastle knowledge add when they discover non-obvious codebase facts not covered by other documentation. Kept it to a single paragraph as the task specified.
went_well: Clean insertion point between documentation and commit phases, where captured knowledge logically belongs: after formal docs, before the commit bundles everything together
improvements: None significant for a single-paragraph prompt addition
action_items: None: the instruction references wolfcastle knowledge add, which is being implemented by sibling tasks in this node
